For case-open path (Noyce NFR-LAT-006), pre-compute hashes during upload (Noyce NFR-LAT-002) and store in fast cache. Monitor hash verification latency; target <200 ms p95 for 100 MB pack. Flag any mismatched hash as security incident requiring immediate escalation to CISO.

security_performance.feature: Audit log write with cryptographic timestamp (RFC 3161 TSA)
security_performance.reasoning: Every panel action (flag, comment, decision change) must be written to immutable distributed audit log with cryptographic timestamp from external RFC 3161 Time Stamp Authority. Noyce NFR-LAT-009 'panel-action log write' budgets 500 ms p95 end-to-end. TSA round-trip adds 80-150 ms. This is non-negotiable for forensic audit trail; incident investigation and Service Inquiry depend on proving exact sequence and timing of decisions. Async TSA calls (fire-and-forget with retry) reduce impact to <50 ms on user-facing latency.
security_performance.recommendation: Use asynchronous TSA call (non-blocking write to user, async timestamping in background). Implement retry logic with exponential backoff if TSA fails. Log TSA failures separately and alert if failure rate exceeds 1%. Use UK-based TSA (e.g., Post Office EDS or equivalent). Monitor TSA latency; set SLA alert if p95 > 120 ms. Store TSA tokens in immutable log for forensic audit.

security_performance.feature: MOR/DPIA redaction on export (REQ012)
security_performance.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-010 'Decision record export' budgets 300 seconds (300,000 ms) for PDF generation. DPIA-driven redaction (removing sensitive personal/witness data from exported decision record) adds significant overhead. Redaction rules are complex: must identify PII patterns (names, addresses, contact info) and redact based on DPIA rules (Data Protection Act 2018 / UK GDPR). PDF rendering with embedded redactions is CPU-intensive. This is REQ012 overhead; cannot be skipped. Export latency p95 66,000 ms suggests 200 ms redaction overhead (67% of typical decision-record component latency).
security_performance.recommendation: Implement redaction as async background process: generate unredacted PDF immediately, stream to temporary storage, redact in background (SLA <30 min for completion). Provide download link with status polling. Cache redaction rules per DPIA determination to avoid re-computation. Monitor DPIA evaluation latency; target <200 ms per rule application. Test with representative 500 MB evidence packs to ensure p95 <300s remains achievable.

security_performance.feature: Applicant credential-stuffing mitigation (rate limiting)
security_performance.reasoning: Applicant portal login endpoint rate-limited to 5 attempts/min per account, 20/min per IP. Rate-limit check is in-memory (Redis) and adds ~1-5 ms. This is a low-latency, high-impact control. Noyce NFR-LAT-001 'Applicant workspace creation' would not be affected by rate limiting on login (login is separate path). Rate limiting is essential for defending against automated credential-stuffing attacks against applicant accounts (civil servants submitting evidence packs).
security_performance.recommendation: Use distributed rate limiter (Redis with Lua scripting for atomic increments). Set rate limits: 5/min per account (account lockout after 3 failures), 20/min per IP. Implement exponential backoff: after 10 failures per account, require CAPTCHA or email verification. Monitor false-positive rate (legitimate users hitting limits); target <0.1%. Log all rate-limit violations and alert on >50 attempts/hour from single IP.

security_performance.feature: Evidence upload size limit enforcement (per-applicant quota)
security_performance.reasoning: Evidence uploads limited to 500 MB per submission (Noyce NFR-LAT-002 typical case is 100 MB) with 2 concurrent uploads per applicant. Concurrent-upload check is enforced at app-server layer (in-memory semaphore) with <2 ms overhead. Prevents evidence-store exhaustion attacks. Noyce NFR-LAT-002 'Evidence upload throughput' assumes network upload is bottleneck (8000 ms); upload-size enforcement is protective gate, not performance bottleneck.
security_performance.recommendation: Enforce size limits at HTTP layer (Content-Length header check). Require pre-approval for >500 MB uploads via CAA case officer. Implement chunked upload with resumption to improve reliability on poor networks. Monitor upload quota violations; alert if >10% of submissions hit limits (may indicate broken client or DDoS).

security_performance.feature: Clause library search rate limiting (100 req/min per user)
security_performance.reasoning: Clause library is public read-only endpoint (OFFICIAL-tier auth gate). Rate limiting 100 requests/min per authenticated user prevents search-based enumeration attacks. Check is O(1) in-memory lookup; <1 ms overhead. Balances usability (100/min = 1.67 searches/second, adequate for human user) against DoS resilience. Global endpoint can handle 10,000 concurrent users at 100 req/min = 16,666 RPS capacity.
security_performance.recommendation: Rate limit at reverse-proxy layer (nginx, HAProxy) for minimal latency impact. Set limit 100/min per user; return HTTP 429 when exceeded. Provide Retry-After header with cooldown. Monitor clause-library search patterns; flag unusual query loads as potential enumeration attack. Whitelist high-volume legitimate users (e.g., CAA staff preparing guidance) with higher limits.

security_performance.feature: Slow-loris detection on classification boundary service
security_performance.reasoning: Classification boundary checker is called for every evidence search (Noyce NFR-LAT-017). Slow-loris attack (attacker sends incomplete HTTP requests to exhaust server connections) could block legitimate searches. Request timeout enforcement (30s read timeout) + connection-timeout tracking adds ~3 ms per request. This is a thin but important protective layer. Attack surface is panel-facing; nation-state adversaries may probe during live panel windows.
security_performance.recommendation: Implement request timeout (30s per HTTP request body). Drop connections idle >5s in request phase. Use nginx/HAProxy connection-limit to cap concurrent connections per IP. Monitor connection state histogram; alert if >5% of connections are in incomplete-request state. Test slow-loris mitigation in security regression suite.

security_performance.feature: DDoS mitigation via CDN + WAF (applicant portal)
security_performance.reasoning: CDN (e.g., Cloudflare, Zenlayer UK) absorbs volumetric DDoS at edge; transparent to origin servers. WAF blocks malicious payloads. Applicant portal can withstand 100k RPS of volumetric DDoS (typical L3/L4 attack) with zero origin impact. No latency overhead for legitimate traffic. This is a critical resilience control given high public-sector attack surface (GCAP/Tempest targets, nation-state probing). Workbench is not expected to handle simultaneous evidence uploads from 100k applicants; DDoS capacity is insurance.
security_performance.recommendation: Contract with UK-sovereign CDN provider offering >=100 Gbps DDoS mitigation. Enable automatic DDoS detection and escalation. Provide manual override for anomaly detection (false positives may block legitimate bulk-upload events). Test DDoS resilience quarterly via red-team exercises. Monitor DDoS incident logs for patterns; escalate nation-state-like attacks to GCHQ.

security_performance.feature: C2 link encryption (TLS 1.3 + QUIC)
security_performance.reasoning: Swarm C2 link (tactical uplink/downlink to aircraft) uses TLS 1.3 over QUIC for low-latency encryption. Noyce NFR-LAT-013 'C2 link latency' budgets 1000 ms p95 end-to-end. QUIC eliminates TCP handshake; TLS overhead is ~2 ms on encrypted packets (cipher suite negotiation is one-time at session start). Airborne autonomy demands tight latency budget; encryption overhead must be minimal.
security_performance.recommendation: Use QUIC transport with TLS 1.3 + AES-256-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305. Pre-compute session keys to avoid negotiation delay. Implement forward secrecy with ephemeral keys. Monitor C2 encryption latency; alert if >5 ms (anomaly). Conduct radio-link fidelity tests to ensure encryption does not introduce packet loss on poor links.

security_performance.feature: Command authentication and integrity verification (HMAC-SHA256)
security_performance.reasoning: Every airborne command from C2 station must be authenticated with HMAC-SHA256 and verified onboard. Verification adds ~3 ms per command on airborne compute (ARM Cortex, not high-end). Noyce NFR-LAT-016 'Flight termination command' budgets 500 ms total; HMAC verification is <3 ms. This is a hard-deadline safety-critical control; skipping verification would allow spoofed commands (e.g., false flight-termination). Adversary might attempt RF spoofing or jamming; authentication proves command came from authorized C2 station.
security_performance.recommendation: Use HMAC-SHA256 with per-session shared keys (rotated hourly). Implement replay detection (sequence numbers) to prevent command replay attacks. Verify HMAC in secure element (SEE) if available on airborne platform. Test command authentication on worst-case compute platforms (ARM Cortex-A53) to ensure <10 ms verification latency. Monitor authentication failures; escalate to C2 officer if >0.1% of commands fail verification.

security_performance.feature: Geofence boundary enforcement with mechanical failsafe
security_performance.reasoning: Geofence boundary check (Noyce NFR-LAT-011) budgets 50 ms for 'geofence_boundary_check' + 100 ms 'decision_logic_execution'. Latitude/longitude comparison + decision logic is ~2 ms on airborne compute. Mechanical failsafe (physical spring-switch on thrust vectoring) activates if GPS is lost. This is REQ005 'automatic containment' control; latency is not primary concern (safety is), but must not degrade control-loop responsiveness.
security_performance.recommendation: Pre-compute geofence zones at mission upload time (avoid runtime math). Use fixed-point arithmetic for GPS comparison to minimize latency. Implement decision logic in deterministic real-time kernel (RTEMS or VxWorks) with bounded execution time. Test geofence enforcement with Monte Carlo simulation of GPS noise/multipath across ODD environment bounds. Conduct flight tests with deliberate geofence boundary violations to verify mechanical failsafe activation.

security_performance.feature: Onboard AI/ML model validation (frozen-model hash verification)
security_performance.reasoning: Onboard ML inference (Noyce NFR-LAT-015, budgets 200 ms p95) must include model validation: SHA-256 hash of frozen model against certified manifest. REQ009 requires 'frozen-model manifest' and forensic explainability. Hash verification adds ~50 ms on airborne compute (single-threaded ARM). This is critical for detecting model tampering or corruption (adversary could attempt to load rogue ML weights). Cannot be skipped.
security_performance.recommendation: Pre-load frozen-model hash into secure element at mission upload. Verify model hash at each inference startup (before first decision). Store certified model manifest in firmware partition (read-only after signed boot). Log any hash mismatch and trigger flight-termination failsafe. Test model validation latency on target hardware (ARM Cortex-A72); ensure <100 ms per verification.

security_performance.feature: Post-incident forensic export (AAIB/Service Inquiry, fast path)
security_performance.reasoning: Incident investigation (AAIB accident, Service Inquiry) requires fast export of decision records + audit logs + ML model frozen-manifest. Simple cases (clear decision, no redaction required) should be exportable within hours, not weeks. Noyce NFR-LAT-010 'Decision record export' is 5 minutes for normal export; forensic export fast-path targets 1 hour for simple cases (5000 ms = 5 seconds per decision within 1-hour window, manageable). Complex cases with extensive redaction may require weeks (classification escalation, witness protection concerns). Not a strict latency requirement, but a forensic-readiness expectation.
security_performance.recommendation: Pre-generate forensic export templates during normal case workflow. Cache decision records + immutable audit logs in fast-export format (protobuf or JSON). Implement fast-path export for simple cases (no redaction, single panelist, <100 MB evidence) with SLA <1 hour. For complex cases, escalate to CISO + legal team (weeks-long manual review may be necessary). Test forensic export latency quarterly with representative case data.

security_performance.feature: Red-team penetration testing (live system, non-disruptive)
security_performance.reasoning: Parent story DoD requires red-team exercises against live Workbench during production operation (no taking system offline). Red-team probes (port scans, fuzzing, SQL injection attempts, credential-stuffing) will hit rate limits and WAF rules, adding latency variation. Synthetic monitoring must remain alert to red-team activity and not mistake it for incident. Expected red-team overhead: 20 ms per probe on average, 1% throughput variance (some probes blocked or delayed). This is operationally acceptable if red-team and SOC are coordinated.
security_performance.recommendation: Schedule red-team exercises during low-volume windows (off-peak hours, weekends if possible). Coordinate with SOC and incident response team. Tag red-team traffic with special User-Agent or X-Red-Team header so SOC can exclude from alerts. Implement synthetic monitoring that remains alert during red-team exercises. After red-team exercise, conduct post-mortem to improve detection and response. Monitor red-team latency impact; ensure p95 latency does not exceed SLA even during exercises.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard-accessible form fields with ARIA labels

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: First impression of tool responsiveness; directly impacts NPS and abandonment rate. Target >60 NPS steady state.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Workspace creation is the entry point for applicants. Noyce budget p95: 3s (NFR-LAT-001). Any delays here trigger abandonment—applicant loses confidence immediately. Must support keyboard-only navigation for users with motor impairments.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Skeleton state for workspace card pre-rendered, optimize auth tier to <200 ms, prioritize rendering_ui component (<400 ms). Ensure form labels auto-announce in screen readers.

usability_metrics.feature: Phase 1 Step 2a: Evidence Upload & RAG Flag Reveal

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA; progress notification must announce every 5 artefact class processed for screen readers

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Time to first meaningful feedback is critical UX metric. Parent story requires 'first RAG flag visible' within <60 s p95. Correlates with perceived responsiveness and perceived progress.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Upload success rate target: p95 >98% on first attempt (network + classification permitting). Noyce budget (NFR-LAT-002): 30 s for 100 MB pack to artefact detector ready. First RAG flag must surface within 60 s of Step 2 complete to prevent abandonment. On 4G (typical for applicants in field), network upload alone budgets 8s, leaving 52s for processing. Progressive reveal is essential.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Implement skeleton coverage matrix during upload. Stream artefact detector results immediately—show RAG flag as soon as first evidence class classified. Use optimistic UI for file receipt. Announce progress every 5 processed classes for accessibility. Test upload path on 4G simulator (10-20 Mbps, variable latency).

usability_metrics.feature: Phase 1 Step 2b: Coverage Matrix Generation & Download

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA; progress bar must update every 60s; downloadable matrix PDF must be accessible (tagged, alt text for charts)

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Time to full coverage matrix is parent story NFR: p95 <10 minutes. Directly correlates with Applicant CSAT. Long waits (>20 min) trigger tool abandonment and lost applications.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Noyce budget (NFR-LAT-003): 10 minutes (600 s) p95 for 100 MB evidence pack. Spans OCR (180s), semantic inference (200s), coverage computation (90s). No intermediate feedback past skeleton renders abandonment risk. Worst-case 500 MB pack budgets 20 minutes (NFR-LAT-018).
usability_metrics.recommendation: Display explicit progress timeline: 'OCR in progress... 2m 30s remaining' with milestones. Progressive reveal of partial matrix as each evidence class completes. For 500 MB worst-case, surface 'Larger pack detected, ~20 min expected' upfront. Ensure PDF matrix includes alt text for coverage heatmap colors. Test on broadband (50+ Mbps) and degraded hotel wifi (5-20 Mbps).

usability_metrics.feature: Phase 1 Step 3: Readiness Dashboard (Operator Feedback Report)

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, high-contrast dashboard cards, screen reader can announce RED/AMBER/GREEN status within <200 ms

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Dashboard render time (Noyce NFR-LAT-004: 5s p99) drives perceived responsiveness of feedback. Slow render frustrates applicants reviewing blocking issues. Impacts abandonment and NPS.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Once coverage matrix is ready, Readiness Dashboard must render in <5s to maintain momentum. Report assembly is app-server responsibility (1.5s budget) with template rendering (0.8s). Accessibility requirement: screen reader users must be able to navigate RED/AMBER/GREEN findings table without exceeding 200ms per row announce.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Pre-fetch dashboard data in background during matrix generation (if possible). Lazy-load non-critical sections (e.g., historical case info). Ensure dashboard table rows are marked as <table role='presentation'> with proper <thead>, <tbody>, <tr> semantics. Test screen reader announce time on matrix tables (10+ rows).

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, pass/fail verdict announced before blocking issues list, keyboard operable form submission

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: End-to-end Phase 1 completion is parent story metric: median <3 working days for well-prepared applicant. Readiness Check is gating operation. Slow checks (<1 min) are acceptable; >3 min blocks perceived progress. Threshold: >3 min → abandonment spike.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Noyce budget (NFR-LAT-005): 95% within 60s, p99 within 80s. Validation engine budgets 25s, cross-reference 15s. Applicant must see PASS/FAIL within 1 minute for confidence. Error rate target: <1% unhandled exceptions reaching Readiness Check.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Render PASS/FAIL verdict first (green checkmark or red X) before listing blocking issues. Progressive list of blocking issues with estimated remediation steps. Announce verdict via ARIA live region for screen readers. Implement timeout handling: if check >120s, surface 'Still checking...' and offer refresh button. Monitor error rate via Readiness Check endpoint logs.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, error messages announced with clear remediation steps, retry button accessible via keyboard

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Upload success rate p95 >98% on first attempt is hard constraint. Every failure requires retry, doubling time-to-matrix. Failed upload drives abandonment. Correlates with quarterly abandonment metric (target <10% of Phase 1 starters).
usability_metrics.reasoning: Network conditions for applicants vary: 4G tethering (10-50 Mbps typical), hotel wifi (5-20 Mbps, variable), enterprise broadband (50+ Mbps). Virus scan + content classification can reject files. Upload errors must be recoverable. Target: 2% error rate (e.g., misclassified file, network timeout during virus scan).
usability_metrics.recommendation: Implement exponential backoff retry with user-visible countdown ('Retrying in 5s...'). Distinguish network errors from classification rejections in UX. For classification rejections, surface: 'This document is marked OFFICIAL. Please check classification level.' Ensure all error messages are announced by screen reader before interactive controls. Test on saturated 4G (variable packet loss).

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard navigation of case list and case panel, screen reader announces case status badges

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Time to case-open (Noyce NFR-LAT-006: 10s p95) is critical for regulator efficiency. Inspectors in multi-day panel sessions open 10-20 cases daily. 10s delay per case = 100-200 s lost per day. Impacts 'Panel productivity metric' and NPS.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Regulators work inside UK sovereign data centre (LAN, low latency) or CAA corporate VPN (moderate latency). Case assembly is app-server responsibility (2s budget) with panel UI rendering (3.9s budget). Classification marking must be visible immediately to prevent accidental disclosure.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Prefetch next 3 likely cases based on case list order (without full rendering). Render skeleton case panel with classification header while evidence metadata loads. Ensure keyboard users can navigate case list via arrow keys and open case via Enter. Test on classified_network simulator (low bandwidth, potential latency spikes).

usability_metrics.feature: Step 5 Continuation: Evidence Navigation (Paragraph Pointer Resolution)

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, screen reader announces section title + paragraph context within <200 ms of navigation

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Evidence-pointer resolution speed (Noyce NFR-LAT-008: 2s p99) is high-frequency operation during panel deliberation. Regulators click 100+ evidence links per case. Slow navigation (<2s) trains cognitive load and error rate. Impacts regulator NPS and decision quality.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Regulator navigates via clauses mapped to evidence paragraphs. Click clause → see paragraph. Network is LAN (low latency), so budget is DOM rendering (0.8s) + JavaScript interaction (0.6s). Must remain snappy despite evidence archive size.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Cache evidence pages once case loaded. Implement hash-based navigation (#clause-123) for instant page jumps without server fetch. Highlight target paragraph with subtle yellow background (max 2s). Announce section title + first 50 chars of paragraph to screen reader immediately. Test with 50+ evidence sections per case.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, narrative flags must be announced progressively as chunks complete; high-contrast toggle for visibility

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: AI-flag narrative (Noyce NFR-LAT-007: 30 min p95) is long-running background operation. Regulators expect 'flags available within 30 min' per parent story. Progressive reveal (chunks completed every 2-3 min) prevents abandonment and maintains engagement.
usability_metrics.reasoning: AI inference spans 900s for chunk processing. One-shot batch operation. Regulators may close case panel or review other cases while waiting. Must surface chunks as they complete, not all-or-nothing at 30 min mark.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Stream narrative flags to panel as inference completes per evidence chunk. Show 'Flag 3 of 8 complete' progress indicator. Allow regulator to begin reviewing partial flags while others compute. If error occurs mid-stream (5% error budget), display 'Retry failed chunks?' with one-click recovery. Ensure progress announcements do not interrupt screen reader flow.

usability_metrics.feature: Step 6: Panel Action Log Write (Comment, Flag, Decision State)

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, optimistic UI shows 'saved' immediately, screen reader announces confirmation without interrupting typing

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Panel action latency (Noyce NFR-LAT-009: 500 ms p95) is perceived-performance critical. Regulators expect instant feedback on comment submission. Slow writes feel broken, even if correct. Under high contention (10+ reviewers), latency can spike to 1s (NFR-LAT-019). Satisfying perceived-performance is key to NPS >50.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Optimistic UI: show 'saved' before distributed log write completes. Roll back only if write fails (rare). Immutable log write budgets 150 ms, replication quorum 100 ms. Under contention, lock acquisition adds 200 ms.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Implement optimistic UI: display 'Your comment saved' immediately. Queue write to distributed log async. If write fails (timeout >2s), show 'Draft saved locally. Sync failed—check connection.' with manual retry. Announce 'Comment recorded' via ARIA live region without interrupting typing. Test with 10-concurrent panel reviewers.

usability_metrics.feature: Step 8: Decision Record Export & PDF Generation

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, exported PDF must be tagged and accessible; progress indicator visible during 5-min export

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Decision record export (Noyce NFR-LAT-010: 5 min p95) is end-of-workflow. Not critical to immediate NPS, but failure to export blocks case closure. Regulator frustration at final step impacts satisfaction. Target: >95% export success rate.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Moderate budget for non-critical path. PDF rendering (100s) + evidence cite resolution (80s) dominate. Signature embedding + encryption (60s) ensure regulatory compliance.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Display progress bar: 'Assembling decision record... 60% complete.' Allow regulator to navigate away while export happens background. Notify via toast when ready: 'Decision record ready—Download now.' Ensure exported PDF includes alt text for all charts and tables. Test on classified_network with large evidence attachments (20+ PDFs).

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, touch-friendly buttons (min 48x48 px), high-contrast text, screen reader compatible

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Mobile usage is rare for applicants (desk-bound), but occasional field check-in on status triggers abandonment if experience is poor. Estimated <5% of Phase 1 workflows touch mobile. Target: graceful degradation, not full feature parity.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Field-range uplink or 3G tethering can drop to <1 Mbps. Upload is impractical; review-only mode is reasonable. Error rate will be higher due to network instability. Timeout handling is critical.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Provide read-only mobile mode: view case status, readiness findings, decision records. Disable uploads on mobile (<50 Mbps detected). Implement optimistic offline mode with local caching. Ensure all buttons meet 48x48 px minimum touch target. Test on 3G simulator with packet loss (10-20%).

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, multi-step form with clear field instructions, progress indicator, keyboard navigable

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: First-time user experience for duty holder (company registration, credentials). Sets tone for tool adoption. Abandonment here (5%) prevents tool use entirely. Impacts organizational NPS.
usability_metrics.reasoning: One-time setup, not performance-critical, but perceived simplicity impacts likelihood of recommending tool to peers. Typical applicants are safety managers and autonomy engineers (non-technical).
usability_metrics.recommendation: Provide step-by-step form wizard with clear labels. Show progress: 'Step 2 of 4: Company Details.' Prefill company lookup via Companies House API. Announce field instructions to screen reader users. Offer 'Save and continue later' option.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, dashboard tables sortable via keyboard, status badges high-contrast

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: RAISO (Regulatory Authority Swarm Inspection Operator) allocates cases to panel chairs. Fast, accurate allocation impacts panel scheduling and regulator satisfaction. Dashboard responsiveness correlates with Regulator NPS.
usability_metrics.reasoning: RAISO works in CAA offices on corporate VPN (moderate latency). Dashboard aggregates case statuses, inbound applications, panel schedules. Must be snappy for daily operations.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Implement sortable columns (click to sort by case ID, applicant, submission date, status). Cache allocation dashboard data, refresh every 10s. Ensure status badges (e.g., 'Awaiting Panel') use color + text (not color-only) for accessibility.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard-operable case list and detailed review; session timeout warning before logout

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Panel session stability and responsiveness drive Regulator NPS and decision quality. Multi-day sessions (2 working days median) with 10+ reviewers require rock-solid UX. Session crashes trigger re-login, frustration, and lost work.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Intense multi-day panel sessions with high-stakes decisions. Must prioritize stability and keyboard operability. Reasonable-adjustments profile: screen reader and high-contrast users attend panels regularly.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Implement session keepalive with visible countdown to timeout (e.g., 'Session expires in 5 min—click here to extend'). Persist case navigation state via session storage. Provide keyboard shortcuts for common panel actions (e.g., Ctrl+S to save decision). Test with 10 concurrent reviewers.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, calibration audit UI for comparing rater responses

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Inter-rater reliability (Cohen's kappa >0.7 on held-out cases) is a regulatory assurance metric, not a user experience metric. But transparent calibration feedback improves regulator confidence in AI flags and panel decision quality.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Quarterly calibration audit with held-out cases. Regulators review same cases independently, then compare verdicts. System must show side-by-side comparison without bias.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Provide anonymized comparison: 'Your decision: GREEN. Panel average: AMBER. AI narrative: RED.' This feedback trains raters without external influence. Ensure UI supports screen reader navigation of comparison tables.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, clear error messages with remediation steps, retry button prominent and accessible

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Error recovery is make-or-break for applicant retention. If applicant encounters error and cannot recover within 5 min, abandonment risk >50%. Impacts quarterly abandonment metric (target <10%).
usability_metrics.reasoning: Evidence upload errors, classification rejections, readiness check failures all require clear error messaging + retry. Network errors on 4G are frequent (10% of uploads may timeout). UX must be forgiving.
usability_metrics.recommendation: Provide specific error message: 'Upload failed: Network timeout. Retrying in 5s...' (not generic 'Error'). Show last successful checkpoint: 'Virus scan complete. Re-uploading classification...' Support manual retry and 'Upload a different file' option. Log all errors for analysis.

usability_performance.accessibility_target: WCAG 2.2 AA, accessible PDF guides, keyboard-navigable FAQ, closed captions on demo video

usability_metrics.satisfaction_correlation: Public-facing documentation drives adoption and reduces support burden. Fast load, clear navigation, accessibility increase perceived professionalism and trust. Not directly measured in NPS but influences tool adoption.
usability_metrics.reasoning: Public site is CDN-served, should be fast. Focus is accessibility and clarity for diverse audience (non-technical stakeholders, accessibility advocates).
usability_metrics.recommendation: Ensure landing page loads <3s on broadband. Provide downloadable accessibility guide (WCAG 2.2 AA PDF). Include FAQ with keyboard navigation support. Embed demo video with auto-generated captions. Link to glossary of regulatory terms.

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 > 2.7s for 5 consecutive minutes
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 3s sustained for 10 minutes OR p99 > 5s for any request
monitoring.alert_incident: workspace creation SLA breach; >1% of requests exceed 5s

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: auto-scale web tier if CPU >70%
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-001 budgets p95 3s, p99 5s. Applicant workspace is entry-point journey; SLA breach triggers abandonment. Synthetic test simulates auth + workspace init + UI render every 60 seconds (OFFICIAL tier path only).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Deploy synthetic test from two edge locations (UK-South, UK-North). Inject synthetic test traffic evenly across day/night to avoid false positives on baseline latency shifts. Alert thresholds: warning at 90% of budget (2.7s), critical at 100% budget.

monitoring_strategy.component: Evidence upload (100 MB pack, per-request RUM)

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 upload latency > 27s (90% of 30s budget) for OFFICIAL tier; trending toward timeout
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 30s OR any request >40s; evidence_upload SLA breach
monitoring.alert_incident: >5% of uploads fail or timeout; application queue depth >100 pending uploads

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: circuit-break if evidence_store write latency >5s; drop new uploads to queue with user-visible retry countdown
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-002 budgets p95 30s for 100 MB pack. Network upload dominates (8s budget). RUM captures real applicant network conditions (4G, hotel wifi variable). Tier-specific: cannot monitor SECRET tier via RUM (no applicant access); OFFICIAL only.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Inject RUM beacon on file upload completion. Segment by network type (broadband vs 4G detected). Track upload throughput (MB/s) as secondary metric. Alert on anomalies in virus-scan latency (>3s suggests malware signature DB issue).

monitoring_strategy.component: Coverage matrix generation (APM on inference tier)

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 matrix generation > 540s (90% of 600s budget); GPU queue depth trending >5
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 600s OR p99 > 720s; matrix SLA breach for 100 MB typical case
monitoring.alert_incident: Inference error rate >2%; GPU worker crashes causing job loss

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: auto-scale GPU workers: add 1 instance if queue depth >20; shed oldest low-priority batch if queue >50
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-003 budgets 600s (10 min) p95 for 100 MB. Spans OCR (180s), semantic classification (200s), matrix computation (90s). APM traces inference component latency. Largest budget component; GPU saturation is key bottleneck. Cold retention for 1 year supports post-trial lessons-learned analysis (cluster similar inference patterns across cases).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Instrument each GPU worker with Prometheus metrics: queue_depth, inference_latency_histogram, ocr_latency, semantic_classification_latency. Alert if any single inference job stalls >180s (watchdog timer). Monitor GPU memory utilization; alert if >90% sustained (batch-size tuning needed).

monitoring_strategy.component: AI-flag narrative service (APM on LLM tier)

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 narrative latency > 1620s (90% of 1800s budget); GPU inference backlog >10 requests
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 1800s; narrative SLA breach OR inference timeout >30s on single chunk
monitoring.alert_incident: LLM model serving crash; inference error rate >5%; regulator complaint about narrative quality

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: auto-scale vLLM instances if batch queue >5 requests; circuit-break token generation if latency spike >50%
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-007 budgets 1800s (30 min) p95 for narrative generation. Evidence chunking (60s) + batch inference (900s) + narrative aggregation (120s) + linking (240s) + rendering (480s). LLM inference is GPU-bottlenecked and tokenization-sensitive. One-shot batch operation per case review; regulators expect 'flags within 30 min'. Streaming token delivery prevents regulator abandonment (partial narratives shown as chunks complete).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Track tokens/second and batch throughput. Monitor model serving framework (vLLM) for OOM errors (context window exhaustion on very large evidence packs). Alert if any single narrative request exceeds 2000s (hard deadline). Log inference errors with chunk ID for root-cause analysis (malformed evidence, encoding issues).

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 case-open latency > 9s (90% of 10s budget) for OFFICIAL tier; regulator perception of slowness begins
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 10s OR p99 > 15s; case-open SLA breach impacts panel productivity
monitoring.alert_incident: Error rate >1% on case-open; cases failing to load entirely (DB or evidence-store outage)

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: add DB read replica if p95 latency >8s; prefetch next 3 likely cases from case list
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-006 budgets 10s p95 for case assembly + UI render. Regulators on classified network (low latency LAN); case-open is high-frequency (~10-20 cases per panel day). RUM captures real regulator experience including network variance. Classification-aware: SECRET tier (air-gapped) will have higher latency; separate SLA (15s acceptable).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Inject RUM beacon on case-open completion. Segment by classification tier (OFFICIAL vs OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE vs SECRET). Track case-assembly latency (app server) vs evidence-metadata-load separately. Alert if >5% of cases fail to load (evidence archive outage signal).

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 navigation latency > 1.8s (90% of 2s budget) during panel sessions; regulator flow interrupted
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 2s sustained; high-frequency interactivity SLA breach
monitoring.alert_incident: >5% of evidence links fail to navigate (cache invalidation issue or broken references)

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: none (UI-side optimization; cache already hot)
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-008 budgets 2s p99 for cached evidence navigation. High-frequency operation during regulator panel work (100+ clicks per case). Cache hit probability must be >95%. DOM rendering dominates (0.8s budget). RUM is essential here; backend latency is not bottleneck.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Monitor cache hit rate per evidence section. Alert if cache hit rate drops <90% (evidence modification during deliberation, or cache eviction due to size pressure). Track DOM rendering time separately (JavaScript profiling on client side); alert if >800ms (browser-side bottleneck).

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 action-log write latency > 450ms (90% of 500ms budget); quorum replication slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 500ms OR any write >750ms; audit-trail SLA breach (REQ012 compliance risk)
monitoring.alert_incident: Log write error rate >0.1%; distributed log replication quorum failure

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: circuit-break on write error; escalate to human ops for manual log recovery
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-009 budgets 500ms p95 for panel-action writes. Immutable audit trail (REQ012) is non-negotiable; any lost action = non-defensible decision. Distributed log (Kafka 3-node quorum) requires replication ACK from 2-of-3 replicas. Low-latency LAN (<10ms) is assumed; WAN failover breaks budget. Multi-year cold retention supports incident investigation and regulatory audit (AAIB/Service Inquiry).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Monitor Kafka broker health: in-sync-replicas count, replication lag, fsync latency. Alert if ISR < 2 (quorum at risk). Track panel action write frequency per panel; expect 30-50 actions/sec peak during voting. Alert if write error rate exceeds 0.01% (1 in 10,000 writes failing).

monitoring.alert_warning: Any classification boundary reject (evidence cannot be classified at target tier); audit escalation required
monitoring.alert_critical: Reject rate > 0.1% of access attempts; possible access-control bypass attempt or data corruption
monitoring.alert_incident: Successful cross-tier access (OFFICIAL user accessing SECRET data); security event

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: block access on any reject; escalate to CISO
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Classification boundary is a hard security control (REQ018). Every tier transition (OFFICIAL → OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE, etc.) must be audited. Rejects indicate either legitimate access denial (expected) or security anomaly. REQ018 absent in original requirements but critical for MOD-involved trials; monitor both success rate and rejection patterns. Classification-marked at highest tier (SECRET) per parent story DoD.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log all tier-transition attempts with success/fail status, user ID, evidence ID, source/target tier. Alert on reject rate spike. Generate daily classification boundary audit report: total transitions, reject counts, user patterns. Test monthly: attempt to access SECRET evidence from OFFICIAL-tier user (should reject instantly).

monitoring.alert_warning: Library revision lag > 7 days (clause update not deployed to CDN edge); regulator using stale guidance
monitoring.alert_critical: Library revision lag > 14 days; regulator risk of applying obsolete clause to decision
monitoring.alert_incident: Library versioning broken; revision ID mismatch between cache and source of truth

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: auto-invalidate CDN cache if source library version advances
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Parent story DoD requires clause-library versioning. Every decision record pins library revision ID at time of decision (audit trail). Drift monitor detects stale clauses in production (inspectors using outdated guidance). Quarterly library updates (3-4 major, 1-2 minor per quarter per Ada capacity model) → freshness check essential. Alert if library revision in production lags source by >7 days (allows for staged rollout, but flags slow propagation).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Track library revision ID in code + evidence store. On library update, measure time to: (1) CDN edge cache, (2) all inspector client caches. Alert if any inspector sees >7-day-old revision. Generate weekly report: library version distribution across inspectors (ensure no old versions in active use).

monitoring.alert_warning: Pass rate declining (>10% relative drop week-over-week); possible regression in applicant evidence quality OR readiness-check validation too strict
monitoring.alert_critical: >50% of submissions fail readiness check on first attempt; validation rules broken OR applicants confused
monitoring.alert_incident: Pass/fail verdict unavailable (readiness engine crashes); submissions stuck in limbo

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: if pass rate drops >30% in 1 hour, escalate to CAA case officers for manual review of recent rejections
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Parent story metric: 'Full workbench open-and-review cycle shall not require more than two working days.' Readiness Check is gate; high failure rate means applicants re-do work (cycle time increases). KPI: track pass % over rolling 7-day window. Pass/fail verdict also logs blocking issues (via telemetry) to understand quality trends (e.g., 'ConOps missing in 40% of submissions' → training opportunity).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Publish daily readiness report: pass/fail counts, median time to gate pass, top-10 blocking issues. Correlate with clause-library revisions (did readiness rule change break applicant expectations?). Quarterly retrospective: analyze submissions that initially failed but later passed (rework effort). Track CSAT: how many applicants report readiness feedback helpful?

monitoring.alert_warning: Regulator override rate on AI flags > 40% over last 10 cases; possible AI model drift or regulator threshold shift
monitoring.alert_critical: Override rate > 60% for single regulator; AI flags unreliable for this reviewer OR calibration training needed
monitoring.alert_incident: Cohen's kappa inter-rater reliability <0.7 on held-out calibration cases; panel decisions not sufficiently aligned (§23 obligation)

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: trigger mandatory calibration session if override rate >40% for individual regulator
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Workbench AI-flag narrative service (NFR-MON-WB-004) generates RED/AMBER/GREEN flags on evidence. Regulators may accept or override. High override rate signals: (1) model drift (evidence distribution changed), (2) regulator threshold shift (acceptance criteria evolved), or (3) model untrained on new case types. REQ009 ML Assurance Case + parent story DoD require calibration loop. Track per-regulator and per-case-type (BVLOS vs autonomy vs swarm, etc.).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log every flag + override decision. Quarterly calibration: present 10 held-out cases to all panelists independently, measure inter-rater agreement (Cohen's kappa). If kappa <0.7, conduct re-training workshop. Generate per-regulator override-rate dashboard: trend line, comparison to panel average, flags where this regulator uniquely differs from peers.

monitoring_strategy.component: Workbench AI components drift (feature + prediction drift)

monitoring.alert_warning: Evidence input distribution shifted (evidence pack composition changing); model operates outside training ODD
monitoring.alert_critical: AI prediction distribution shifted >20% (e.g., flag rate was 30%, now 50%); model retraining required
monitoring.alert_incident: Model confidence dropped <60% median; predictions unreliable

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: hold inference results pending review if confidence <60% on any evidence chunk
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Parent story DoD: 'Workbench AI components drift monitor.' Coverage matrix engine (NFR-MON-WB-003) and narrative service (NFR-MON-WB-004) are ML-based. Monitor: (1) input distribution (evidence content), (2) model outputs (flag distribution), (3) confidence scores. Drift signals retrain requirement. GCAP/Tempest trials may introduce new evidence types (advanced autonomy scenarios) → model must adapt.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Collect feature statistics per case: document types (PDF vs. video), evidence length distribution, clause topic distribution. Monthly: compare to training-set baseline. Alert if Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p-value < 0.05 (distribution shift detected). Track model confidence percentiles (p50, p90, p99). If p50 confidence trends down >10% month-over-month, trigger retraining evaluation.

monitoring_strategy.component: DAA encounter rate and resolution latency

monitoring.alert_warning: Encounter resolution latency > 4.5s (90% of 5s budget for cooperative encounters); close to safety margin
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 encounter latency > 5s for cooperative OR > 27s for non-cooperative (DO-365 compliance risk)
monitoring.alert_incident: Encounter detection failure (sensor fusion miss); swarm did not respond to nearby aircraft

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: escalate to trial safety officer if encounter latency >3s (early warning)
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-012 budgets 5s p95 for cooperative DAA encounter resolution, 30s for non-cooperative. Safety-critical: collision avoidance. APM instruments ground-control state machine (sensor fusion latency, consensus coordination, command generation). Per-event logging captures every encounter for post-trial forensic analysis. Retains for 7 years (regulatory investigation support).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log all DAA events: detected aircraft type (cooperative vs non-cooperative), bearing, range, altitude, resolution maneuver, latency. Track encounter rate per trial (1-2/hour typical per Ada capacity). Alert if any encounter takes >6s to resolve (>20% over budget margin). Monthly: analyze encounter resolution patterns; identify any trends in latency.

monitoring_strategy.component: C2 link SNR, jitter, packet loss

monitoring.alert_warning: Packet loss rate > 2% over 1-minute window (elevated but recoverable); C2 robustness tested
monitoring.alert_critical: Packet loss > 5% sustained for 10 seconds OR SNR < 3 dB; trial safety margin eroded
monitoring.alert_incident: C2 link loss detected; autonomous survival mode triggered

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: activate C2 link redundancy (satellite backup) if loss rate >5% for >5 sec
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-013 budgets 1000ms p95 C2 round-trip. Packet loss and jitter directly degrade latency. Monitor RF link quality (SNR via SDR telemetry, jitter via timestamp analysis). LTE/5G networks are inherently variable; alert thresholds reflect acceptable operational envelope per REQ006. Link loss triggers autonomous survival (geofence hold, RTL).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log SNR, RSSI, packet loss rate every 100ms during trial. Plot C2 link quality timeline per trial (identify geographic dead zones, time-of-day patterns). Alert if SNR drops <0 dB (link marginal). Correlate packet loss with encounter events (busy periods may see higher loss due to bandwidth contention). Post-trial: generate C2 link quality report for trial debrief.

monitoring.alert_warning: Any aircraft distance-to-geofence-edge < 100m; approaching boundary (late-stage warning before breach)
monitoring.alert_critical: Distance-to-edge < 50m for any aircraft in swarm; geofence containment margin very tight
monitoring.alert_incident: Geofence breach detected (GPS position outside authorized flight envelope); automatic containment triggered

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: automatic containment maneuver if breach occurs (mechanical failsafe per REQ005)
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: REQ005 'Automatic Containment' requires geofence monitoring. Distance-to-edge distribution reveals: (1) swarm flying too close to boundary (operational risk), (2) GPS noise (multipath errors), (3) dynamic geofence shrinkage (MOD range safety requirement). Monitor per-vehicle and swarm-average distance. Alert on breach (emergency condition requiring incident response).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log GPS position + calculated distance-to-edge every 1 second. Generate per-trial geofence histogram: % time at <50m, <100m, <500m from boundary. Alert if any vehicle distance < 0 (impossible, signals GPS/geofence mismatch). Post-trial: overlay flight path on authorized geofence map; identify any close-calls or boundary-hugging behavior (training feedback for pilot).

monitoring.alert_warning: Consensus cycle duration trending >150ms (90% of 200ms budget); coordination protocol slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: Consensus cycle > 200ms OR any aircraft consensus failure (node isolation); swarm decision-making stalled
monitoring.alert_incident: Consensus quorum lost (<50% of swarm synchronized); formation breaks or returns to manual C2

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: revert to manual C2 control if consensus quorum lost >10 seconds
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-014 budgets 200ms p95 for swarm consensus. Inter-vehicle coordination via gossip/broadcast; consensus time increases with swarm size (O(n²) pairwise comparisons). Monitor: (1) cycle duration per decision heartbeat, (2) agreement rate (% of vehicles reached consensus), (3) decision latency (time from sensor input to coordinated action). Consensus failure = swarm splits into independent actors (loss of coordinated autonomy).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log consensus start/end timestamp, participating aircraft count, decision output (e.g., 'all maintain formation' vs 'Aircraft-2 break right'). Alert if cycle duration exceeds 250ms (timeout margin). Monthly: analyze consensus performance across different swarm sizes (5 vs 10 vehicles); verify linear-or-better scaling. Test consensus resilience: simulate single aircraft communication loss, measure recovery time.

monitoring_strategy.component: Battery state of charge and predicted time-to-contingency

monitoring.alert_warning: Predicted time-to-RTL < 10 minutes for any aircraft (margin tightening); crew plan recovery soon
monitoring.alert_critical: Predicted time-to-RTL < 5 minutes OR battery < 20% SOC; imminent forced landing risk
monitoring.alert_incident: Battery completely depleted mid-flight; aircraft unable to execute RTL (emergency recovery)

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: auto-trigger RTL if time-to-contingency < 3 minutes
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Safety-critical: battery depletion ends flight abruptly. Model discharge rate in real-time accounting for compute load (autonomy + DAA inference), wind conditions, vehicle weight. Predict time-to-contingency: time remaining to reach safe landing zone from current position. Alert if crew cannot execute planned recovery (forces early RTL). Per-vehicle monitoring; also swarm-level alert if any vehicle is contingency-limited (bottleneck for swarm).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log battery SOC, voltage, current draw every 1 second per aircraft. Calculate discharge rate (amps per hour); compare to pre-flight plan. Alert if actual discharge >10% above predicted (thermal throttling, headwind, or load increase). Generate per-trial battery report: actual vs predicted discharge, time-to-RTL history, any near-contingency events. Post-flight: debrief on power management (did crew manage intelligently, or was power budget misestimated?).

monitoring_strategy.component: Onboard ML inference latency and confidence distribution

monitoring.alert_warning: Onboard ML p95 latency > 180ms (90% of 200ms budget); approaching autonomy-decision latency budget
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 ML latency > 200ms OR inference error rate > 2%; autonomy decision timing at risk
monitoring.alert_incident: Model inference crash; autonomy fallback activated (manual C2 or pre-programmed behavior)

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: revert to fallback behavior if inference latency >250ms or confidence <50%
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-015 budgets 200ms p95 for onboard ML inference (e.g., next-waypoint selection, DAA evasion). Jetson Orin or similar provides GPU inference; onboard compute is TDP-constrained. Monitor: (1) latency per inference call, (2) model confidence (%), (3) inference error rate (exceptions/NaN outputs). Confidence <60% signals ODD boundary: model operating on unseen inputs.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log every ML inference: input features, latency, output, confidence score. Alert if latency >250ms (hard deadline for control loop). Track confidence distribution per flight (histogram). Alert if median confidence trends down (model degradation). Post-trial: cluster inference examples by confidence to identify which scenarios model is uncertain about (training data needs).

monitoring.alert_warning: State machine p95 latency > 90ms (90% of 100ms budget); swarm decision validation slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 100ms OR state-machine error rate > 0.5%; autonomy compliance validation broken
monitoring.alert_incident: State machine crash or stall; ground control loses real-time autonomy visibility

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: escalate to human operator (trial abort) if state machine unresponsive >5 seconds
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: REQ008 Swarm Decision-Making requires real-time ground validation: did swarm comply with authorized flight plan? Ground-control state machine processes telemetry from swarm (25-200 msg/sec), validates each message against authorized plan, logs state transitions. Latency budget: <100ms (must complete validation before next message arrives). Critical for regulatory oversight (CAA/MOD must prove swarm did not deviate).
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Log state machine latency per telemetry message (histogram). Track state-machine CPU utilization; alert if >80% (approaching capacity). Alert if validation error rate exceeds 1 per 10,000 messages (1-in-10k validations fail). Monthly: analyze state-machine logs for compliance violations (instances where swarm deviated from authorized plan; require human investigation).

monitoring.alert_warning: Evidence store GetObject latency > 90ms (trending toward 100ms SLA); S3 performance degrading
monitoring.alert_critical: GetObject latency > 100ms p95; metadata retrieval for case assembly may miss SLA
monitoring.alert_incident: Evidence store unavailable (bucket access denied or S3 outage); cases cannot load

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: activate CloudFront cache for frequently accessed evidence; pre-warm before panel sessions
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: NFR-LAT-006 case-open budgets 1500ms for evidence-metadata-load. S3 GetObject latency is part of that. Monitor object-storage read performance per tier (OFFICIAL faster, SECRET/STRAP may be slower due to encryption/compliance). Track storage growth (34-month projection: 140 GB per Ada capacity). Availability is high; focus is on latency consistency.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Monitor S3 bucket metrics: GetObject latency p50/p95/p99, PutObject latency, request counts. Alert if GetObject p95 > 100ms sustained. Track storage utilization growth; ensure growth projections remain on track (may reveal unexpected case volume). Test quarterly: simulate S3 partition (brief degradation); verify graceful fallback and recovery.

monitoring_strategy.component: Primary database (write throughput and read latency)

monitoring.alert_warning: Write latency p95 > 400ms (trending toward 500ms panel-action-write budget); master becoming bottleneck
monitoring.alert_critical: Write latency p95 > 500ms sustained OR write error rate > 0.1%; SLA breach for audit trail
monitoring.alert_incident: Database unavailable; cases cannot be loaded or decisions recorded

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: add DB read replica if read latency >100ms; scale master vertically if write latency >400ms
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: NFR-LAT-009 panel-action log write budgets 500ms p95. Database master handles all writes (audit trail, case state, panel decisions). Monitor: (1) write TPS (transactions/second), (2) write latency percentiles, (3) replication lag to read replicas, (4) lock-contention events. At 100 concurrent users, expect 80 write TPS + 400 read TPS.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Enable CloudWatch/DataDog monitoring: WriteThroughput, ReadThroughput, WriteLatency, ReadLatency, DatabaseConnections. Alert if WriteLatency p95 > 400ms (warning) or >500ms (critical). Track replication lag; alert if lag >1s (read replicas becoming stale). Monthly: analyze slow-query logs; identify any N+1 query patterns or missing indexes.

monitoring_strategy.component: Web front-end and BFF (request latency and error rate)

monitoring.alert_warning: Frontend p95 latency > 1800ms (trending toward 2s applicant usability threshold); DOM rendering or JS execution slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 2000ms sustained OR error rate > 1%; user-facing quality degradation
monitoring.alert_incident: Frontend service unavailable (CDN or origin down); applicants cannot access portal

monitoring_strategy.mitigation_automation: auto-scale web tier if CPU >70% OR request rate >500 req/s per instance
monitoring_strategy.reasoning: RUM monitors real user experience: download, parsing, rendering, JavaScript execution. Noyce NFR-LAT-001 budgets 3s p95 end-to-end for applicant workspace creation; frontend is part of that. Monitor per-page and per-feature: upload page, dashboard, evidence nav, etc. Applicant portal is high-attack-surface (public-facing); error rate may indicate WAF blocking or DDoS.
monitoring_strategy.recommendation: Inject RUM beacon on page load complete. Segment by device type (mobile vs desktop), network speed (broadband vs 4G). Track Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS). Alert if error rate > 1% (HTTP 4xx/5xx from origin). Monitor CDN cache hit rate; alert if <90% (cache churn or expired TTL).

acceptance_criteria: p95 latency drift <5% over 24h at 80% peak load (applicant workspace creation latency p95 starts <3s, must remain <3.15s)
p95 latency drift <5% over subsequent 72h at 50% steady-state (15 concurrent users)
zero unhandled exceptions in application layer logs
database connection pool utilization stable (<80% of 40-connection budget)
audit log write latency p95 stable (<500ms) throughout 96h
log file handle count stable (<100 open handles on primary app instance)
test_strategy.reasoning: Applicant intake and inspector review are continuous workloads. Evidence store metadata indexing degradation is a known risk (ada-capacity.json NFR-CAP-WB-001: 'object metadata index causing search degradation'). Panel deliberation log is append-only write-heavy; drift in write latency indicates ledger fragmentation or connection pool leaks. 96-hour soak spans business cycles (24h at peak, 72h at steady-state) to detect circadian degradation patterns. Memory leaks in GPU inference (AI narrative flags) must be verified during long runs.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute in pre-prod environment with 20% production data volume (10 GB evidence store replica). Use load generator to simulate 20 applicant users uploading 100-200 MB packs over 24h, then 50% steady-state for 72h. Instrument: collect JVM heap metrics, database connection pool metrics, audit log write latency histograms. Capture database slow query logs (>100ms). Alert threshold: if p95 latency exceeds 3.15s or connection pool >35/40, pause and investigate. Post-test: analyze memory growth trends (linear = leak, flat = clean).

acceptance_criteria: system degrades gracefully: queues requests instead of rejecting (coverage matrix job queue depth acceptable; new submissions queue in Redis, not rejected)
p95 latency increases to <6s (acceptable 2x degradation from 3s baseline)
zero crash failures in app server layer
database master latency <1s p95 (write consistency maintained)
after load drops, p95 latency returns to baseline within 15 minutes
panel-mode sessions shed gracefully (reject new panel opens at capacity, but maintain existing sessions without mid-session drops)
test_strategy.reasoning: Liskov scalability (NFR-SCL-WB-001) identifies breaking points: web frontend breaks at 500-800 req/s; app server (coverage matrix) at ~1 concurrent inference; database at 150 write TPS + 500 read TPS. Stress test 2x peak to verify graceful degradation and recovery. Applicant workflow tolerates queue delays (asynchronous); inspector workflow cannot tolerate mid-session drops. Classification tier transitions (SECRET evidence ingestion burst) can trigger 2x spike; system must not deny service.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute in staging environment with production-like infrastructure (load balancer + 3 app instances). Ramp load to 2x peak over 10 minutes (gradual stress). Monitor: database CPU, app server memory, coverage matrix job queue depth, active HTTP connections. Deliberately trigger 5x peak for 30s (spike scenario) and measure recovery. Post-stress: verify no corrupted transactions, no orphaned sessions, database consistency check passes.

endurance.load_multiplier: 3x peak (sudden wave of 75 concurrent applicants, all uploading simultaneously)
endurance.data_volume_projection: intake surge ~5-10 GB evidence ingest in 5 minutes

acceptance_criteria: spike event: Quarterly panel cycle start (sudden submission wave per ada-capacity.json 'Quarterly panel cycle start — sudden wave of applicant submissions'), Clause-library publication (burst of re-checks), Incident day (re-reads of historical decisions)
system absorbs 75 concurrent uploads without rejecting; queueing acceptable
p95 latency spikes to <10s (transient, acceptable for batch scenario)
evidence store throughput >3 MB/s sustained for 5 minutes (upload rate spec per Noyce NFR-LAT-002)
recovery: within 10 minutes of spike drop, system returns to baseline
zero incomplete uploads (all-or-nothing semantics for evidence packs)
test_strategy.reasoning: Spike tests known burst events. Quarterly panel cycles initiate waves of re-submissions. Clause library publications trigger re-assessment of in-flight applications (evidence search + re-checking). Incident days (accident investigations) trigger historical evidence re-reads. System must sustain 3x peak for 15 minutes without dropping requests; queue-based architecture (Redis) provides backpressure.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute spike test for each known event: (1) Clause-library publication: simulate 1000+ historical cases each requiring re-check (search + coverage matrix). (2) Incident day: simulate 100+ concurrent analysts querying same evidence (read-heavy). (3) Quarterly submission wave: simulate 75 new applicant uploads all arriving within 5-minute window. Monitor: evidence store PUT latency histogram, evidence store object count growth, database transaction latency p95, coverage matrix job queue depth. Measure recovery time from peak load drop.

endurance.load_multiplier: 1x → 10x peak (binary search for component breaking point)

acceptance_criteria: identify GPU inference breakpoint: single GPU instance breaks at ~1 concurrent inference job (per Liskov NFR-SCL-WB-002); plan auto-scale to +1 GPU worker when queue depth >20 items

identify SECRET classification tier breakpoint: air-gapped instance cannot scale; single SECRET DB replica (per Liskov NFR-SCL-WB-004); rebuild latency 2-4 hours on failure (unacceptable; mitigation: never scale SECRET tier horizontally, reserve 3 dedicated instances or trigger incident escalation)

identify swarm consensus breakpoint (NFR-SCL-SWM-002): pairwise de-confliction O(n²) scales to 10-vehicle swarm (~45 pairwise comparisons, ~200ms compute); 20-vehicle swarm exceeds 500ms latency SLA (migration: hierarchical clustering or distributed consensus)
add 50% safety margin to auto-scale triggers: if GPU inference breaks at 1 concurrent, trigger scale-out at 0.5 concurrent (pre-scale before hitting ceiling)
test_strategy.reasoning: Breakpoint tests identify exact capacity limits per Liskov scalability. Allows precision auto-scale tuning. GPU inference (coverage matrix, AI narrative) is bottleneck; batching 5-10 jobs per GPU improves efficiency. Classification tier segregation creates separate bottlenecks (SECRET tier cannot scale). Swarm consensus complexity O(n²) means multi-vehicle swarms hit soft capacity limits.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute binary search: start at 1x peak, increment by +1x peak every 2 minutes. Stop when latency exceeds SLA. Record: job queue depth, GPU memory utilization, p95 latency vs load. Repeat for each component (coverage matrix, AI narrative, evidence search). Map results to Liskov NFR-SCL values. Implement auto-scale rules with 50% safety margin (scale at half the observed breaking point). For classification tiers: accept that SECRET tier is capacity-constrained; document incident escalation procedure if load approaches 85% of single-instance capacity.

endurance.data_volume_projection: 10-year projection: 140 GB evidence store, 5 GB clause library, 1.5 GB audit trail

acceptance_criteria: Evidence store metadata query (NFR-LAT-017): p95 search latency remains <5s across 10-year volume projection (140 GB, 10M+ objects)
Clause library search (NFR-LAT-004): p95 latency <500ms at 10-year volume (5 GB, 4 GB cumulative versions retained)
Audit log ledger scan/export (REQ020): export of 10-year audit trail (1.5 GB, ~1M entries at 15 panels/year × 100 actions/panel) completes within Noyce latency budget
Index fragmentation: evidence store object listing latency does not degrade >10% from fresh baseline
Database query plan regression: queries that execute in <100ms at 2GB data volume remain <150ms at 140GB (acceptable 50% growth due to index bloat)
test_strategy.reasoning: Ada capacity model projects 36-month evidence store at 140 GB (NFR-CAP-WB-005). Clause library accumulates versions (5-year retention = ~4 GB). Audit logs grow 1.5 GB over 10 years. Volume tests verify indexing strategy scales; metadata query performance on object inventory is known risk (ada-findings.json 'slow growth of object metadata index causing search degradation'). Search must remain interactive (<5s) even at 10M objects.
test_strategy.recommendation: Populate test database with 10-year data projection: use production data sanitization (PII redaction) to create 140 GB evidence store replica. Run benchmark queries: (1) evidence search by keyword across 10M objects, (2) clause library full-text search, (3) audit log export for single case (15 years of history per case in steady state). Measure: query latency histogram, index size, query plan cost (EXPLAIN ANALYZE). Monitor: database page cache hit rate (target >95%). If query latency degrades >10%, trigger index optimization (REINDEX, statistics refresh). Post-test: validate export correctness (row count, checksum).

acceptance_criteria: GPU inference memory watermark <85% on 72h soak; no fragmentation or memory leaks detected
Database connection pool utilization: peak 35/40, steady-state 15/40; zero connection leak (monitor per-hour statistics for upward drift)
Evidence store object listing latency: stable <2s for listing 100k objects; no drift over 72h
Panel action log write latency: p95 stable at 500ms baseline; standard deviation <100ms (no variance creep)
AI narrative inference latency: p95 stable (completion time for single narrative inference batch does not drift >5% over 72h)
test_strategy.reasoning: Endurance profile examines degradation over long durations. GPU inference (coverage matrix + AI narrative) can leak memory if model serving framework (vLLM, TensorRT) has unfixed bugs. Database connection pool leaks manifest as steady drift in active-connection count. Evidence store metadata index growth can slow object listing if not optimized. Panel action log (immutable append-only ledger) can drift if fsync latency increases with ledger size. Applicant workflow (submission intake) is low-latency sensitive; 72h soak verifies no ambient latency creep.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute 72-hour soak at 1x peak load (20 applicant + 36 inspector concurrent users). Instrument: collect detailed metrics every 5 minutes: GPU memory (nvidia-smi), database connection pool active count, evidence store object-listing query latency, distributed log write latency, AI narrative inference latency. Build time-series plot for each metric; analyze for linear drift or step-function jumps. Thresholds: if GPU memory exceeds 85%, or connection pool active >35, or object listing latency drifts >5%, trigger alert and pause test. Post-test: analyze correlation between metrics (e.g., does object listing latency drift correlate with database CPU load?).

endurance.load_multiplier: 2x peak panel concurrency (20 concurrent panelists, 3 simultaneous panels)

acceptance_criteria: Panel action log write (NFR-LAT-009): p95 latency under contention (10 simultaneous panelists) must not exceed 1000ms (elevated from 500ms baseline per NFR-LAT-019); zero action loss
Classification boundary checks (NFR-LAT-005): during high-contention panel write phase, classification marking latency remains <150ms (tight envelope)
Distributed ledger quorum writes: 2-of-3 replicas ACK within 150ms (latency envelope); zero divergence between replicas
Panel session state machine: concurrent case-opens (panelists opening different cases) do not interfere; case state remains consistent
test_strategy.reasoning: Panel deliberation is high-stakes low-concurrency workload. Stress test simulates 2x expected peak: 10-20 concurrent panelists (vs 8-12 baseline) making simultaneous annotations on same case or different cases. Contention can spike distributed log write latency (lock acquisition, quorum ACK delays). Classification boundary service must remain responsive even during high write load (audit trail must be immutable and timestamped).
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute in pre-prod with dual panel sessions running in parallel (3 concurrent panels × 6-7 panelists each = 18-21 concurrent users simulated). Synchronize: trigger simultaneous annotations from all panelists (coordinated load spike). Monitor: audit log write latency histogram, classification boundary service response time, distributed ledger replica consistency. Verify: no action lost, no duplicate actions, ledger has monotonic sequence numbers. Post-test: audit log integrity check (verify all actions present, in order, with valid timestamps).

endurance.load_multiplier: 1x sustained (5-10 vehicle swarm nominal telemetry rate 5-10 Hz per vehicle)
endurance.data_volume_projection: 1.5-3 GB telemetry per trial (90-120 min flight)

acceptance_criteria: C2 link latency (NFR-LAT-013): p95 round-trip <1000ms (tactical) sustained for 4 hours; no single-packet loss events >2 consecutive packets
Autonomy state machine (NFR-LAT-014): swarm consensus cycle latency p95 <200ms for 4-hour duration; decision heartbeat remains synchronized across all aircraft
DAA sensor fusion (NFR-LAT-012): encounter detection and response latency p95 <5000ms for cooperative encounters; zero false-negative detections
Telemetry logging: evidence stream to ground-control database remains unbroken; no gaps >1 second in telemetry records
Geofence enforcement (NFR-LAT-011): geofence boundary check latency stable <500ms; zero boundary violations logged
test_strategy.reasoning: Swarm endurance differs from workbench: real aircraft have limited battery (2-4 hour missions). Telemetry is continuous high-frequency stream (50-100 msg/sec for 5-vehicle swarm). C2 link fading (RF interference, shadowing) can trigger autonomy survival behavior. Sensor fusion complexity grows with swarm size. Ground control segment must sustain <100ms state-machine latency for 4 hours without degradation (if latency drifts, encounter resolution becomes untimely).
test_strategy.recommendation: Conduct endurance flight test: launch 5-10 vehicle swarm for sustained 90-120 minute flight (saturate available battery capacity). Simulate: (1) One cooperative encounter mid-flight (DAA + coordination response). (2) Transient C2 link fade (30-60 second RF blackout, trigger autonomous hold + RTL). (3) Continuous telemetry logging to ground-control database + evidence archive. Monitor: C2 link latency histogram (real RF measurements), swarm consensus decision lag across aircraft, telemetry gap detection. Post-flight: analyze telemetry stream for consistency and completeness. Verify all encounter events logged with timestamps.

endurance.load_multiplier: 2x vehicle count (10-20 vehicle swarm at high decision frequency)

acceptance_criteria: DAA encounter rate: 10+ encounters per 15-minute window; system resolves each within p95 <5s (cooperative) per NFR-LAT-012

Swarm consensus latency (NFR-LAT-014): pairwise de-confliction decision latency p95 <200ms for 10-vehicle swarm; for 20-vehicle swarm, acknowledge graceful degradation (target <300ms, acceptable >500ms indicates need for hierarchical clustering)
C2 uplink bandwidth: sustained 2-3 Mbps for 20-vehicle swarm (100 kbps per vehicle + coordination overhead); no packet loss >1% (RF fading simulator)
Autonomy model inference (NFR-LAT-015): onboard ML inference latency p95 <200ms during encounter-resolution decision cycles
Recovery: after stress window, swarm formation re-establishes and consensus synchronizes within 30 seconds
test_strategy.reasoning: Swarm stress tests multi-vehicle coordination under high encounter rates and decision load. DAA sensor fusion is CPU-intensive onboard (fusion algorithm, pairwise comparisons). Swarm consensus is O(n²) in vehicle count; 20 vehicles = 190 pairwise comparisons per consensus cycle. C2 bandwidth is radio-spectrum constraint (500 kbps per vehicle typical for LTE; 20 vehicles = 10 Mbps, unrealistic for single ground station). Stress scenario validates coordination complexity and identifies bottlenecks.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute high-encounter-rate flight test: pre-program 10+ cooperative aircraft targets (via ADS-B simulator) to create sustained encounter scenarios over 15-minute window. Measure: DAA detection time per encounter, resolution latency (time to coordinated maneuver completion), C2 uplink bandwidth utilization, swarm consensus lag. For 20-vehicle simulation: use numerical simulation (not live flight) to evaluate pairwise de-confliction latency; compare to 500ms SLA. Document: at what vehicle count does latency exceed budget? Recommendation: if 20-vehicle swarm consensus latency exceeds 500ms, implement hierarchical clustering (sub-swarms internally resolve, parent consensus at cluster level).

acceptance_criteria: C2 command authentication (NFR-SEC-016): HMAC-SHA256 verification latency <3ms per command; zero false-positive rejections (valid commands rejected)
Onboard model validation (NFR-SEC-018): frozen-model hash verification latency <50ms at mission startup; model tampering detected (hash mismatch) triggers flight-termination failsafe
Geofence enforcement (NFR-SEC-017, NFR-LAT-011): boundary check latency <50ms; geofence violation triggers containment action within 500ms; zero boundary overshoots
Flight termination (NFR-LAT-016): command authentication + motor shutdown within 500ms hard deadline; zero false negatives (invalid termination commands rejected)
Red-team probe resilience: system does not degrade p95 latency >5% even during concurrent red-team probes (spoofed commands, model injection attempts, geofence tampering)
test_strategy.reasoning: Security-performance integration test (Gate 7) verifies that security controls (authentication, model validation, geofence checks) do not degrade latency or create new exploitable timing channels. Red-team can probe during active load to simulate adversary attempting to exploit system under stress. Flight termination is safety-critical; authentication overhead must not delay termination.
test_strategy.recommendation: Execute 1-hour live swarm flight + concurrent red-team exercises: (1) Red-team attempts spoofed C2 commands (wrong HMAC); system rejects with <3ms overhead. (2) Red-team attempts to inject rogue model weights; hash verification detects tampering, triggers failsafe. (3) Red-team attempts geofence boundary tampering (GPS spoofing simulator); system detects violation and triggers RTL. (4) Nominal flight continues while red-team is active. Measure: p95 latencies for all security operations; compare to baseline. Post-test: analyze red-team success rate (expected zero successful exploits; non-zero indicates security flaw or performance-latency issue).

endurance.data_volume_projection: twin-sample: Scotland case + SORA case (opposite ends of complexity spectrum)

acceptance_criteria: End-to-end twin-sample journey: each case (Scotland PDRA-01 VLOS, SORA advanced swarm) completes within Noyce SLA budgets: (1) Workspace creation <3s p95, (2) Evidence upload + classification <30s p95 for 100 MB pack, (3) Coverage matrix <10 min p95, (4) Readiness check <60s p95, (5) Panel case-open <10s p95, (6) AI narrative flags within 30 min p95, (7) Decision record export <5 min p95
